lanes.name: Customer analytics reservation lane
lanes.narrative: Customer analytics has enough predictable slot demand to justify a commitment packet if idle controls stay enforced.
lanes.nextAction: Package a one-year slot commitment with utilization, owner, and savings evidence.

lanes.name: Revenue close reporting lane
lanes.narrative: Revenue close has high criticality and a stable month-end shape, but reservation coverage needs rightsizing first.
lanes.nextAction: Move recurring close jobs into a finance-owned reservation and cap ad hoc queries.

lanes.name: Growth experimentation lane
lanes.narrative: Experiment workloads spike around launches and are not yet predictable enough for broad commitments.
lanes.nextAction: Keep on-demand, add budget alerts, and separate launch bursts from recurring experiment analysis.

lanes.name: Security telemetry enrichment lane
lanes.narrative: Security telemetry is important, but bursty enrichments should be isolated before a commitment decision.
lanes.nextAction: Right-size a small protected reservation and keep high-burst enrichment jobs outside the commitment pool.

lanes.name: Product exploration sandbox
lanes.narrative: Product exploration is valuable, but it should not be converted into committed spend without stronger ownership.
lanes.nextAction: Stay on-demand, archive idle tables, and require owner tags before any reservation allocation.
